terms.label: Structural Fingerprint
terms.definition: A neutral representation of fund structure and knowledge state; never a quality score.

terms.label: Verifiable Classification Memory
terms.definition: A versioned record of what was known, what changed, and why a classification judgment changed.

terms.label: Exposure Change Intelligence
terms.definition: The governed analysis of what changed inside a fund, when, how it is known, and whether classification changed.

terms.label: Decision Transparency
terms.definition: Publication of the rule, evidence, inference, limitations, status, and review date behind a classification.

terms.label: Disclosure Freshness
terms.definition: The relationship between a source's relevant date, its retrieval, and the applicable review window.

terms.label: Structural Proximity
terms.definition: Similarity across governed structural dimensions without implying quality or suitability.

terms.label: Evidence Admission
terms.definition: The protocol-controlled acceptance of bounded evidence into the public registry.

terms.label: Classification Change Event
terms.definition: An event-based record that preserves the previous value, new value, trigger, cause, and standard version.

terms.label: Unresolved Exposure State
terms.definition: An explicit state used when reviewed evidence is incomplete, unknown, or conflicting.

terms.label: Implementation Route
terms.definition: The governed description of instruments and legal structures used to obtain exposure.

terms.label: Market Breadth
terms.definition: The set of market families within a strategy's evidenced opportunity universe.

terms.label: Signal Horizon
terms.definition: The evidenced time horizon over which strategy signals are formed or expressed.

terms.label: Review Window
terms.definition: The governed interval after which evidence or a decision requires renewed review.

terms.label: Evidence Sufficiency
terms.definition: Whether admitted evidence satisfies the minimum requirements of a named ECS rule.
